steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure of the subject
steps.content: The subject is the noun phrase “Stephanie Shonekan of the University of Missouri, along with researchers George Starks of Drexel University and Tyron Cooper of Indiana University.” The main noun is singular, and the phrase “along with researchers” is a prepositional phrase that provides additional information without changing the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply the agreement rule
steps.content: A verb must match the number of its subject. 'Serves' is singular and is the only choice that maintains agreement. The other choices (are serving, have served, serve) are all plural. That matches choice C — C is correct.
